Mexico City.propertyNote: Central boroughs (Roma, Condesa, Polanco) low theft; metro-wide petty crime
Mexico City.womensSafetyNote: Gallup MX women feel-safe low; central districts patrolled
Mexico City.touristRationale: Central tourist boroughs far safer than the citywide figure; main visitor risks are petty theft and express kidnapping via unregulated taxis.

_audit.note: womensAdj -11 preserves pre-WPS hand-set womensSafety 42 vs country Gallup baseline 53 (CS 41%)

San Miguel de Allende.propertyNote: Walkable expat core, very low street theft
San Miguel de Allende.womensSafetyNote: Expat community reports feeling safe; statewide women's safety low
San Miguel de Allende.touristRationale: Guanajuato's Bajío cartel war is intra-criminal and concentrated in peripheral neighborhoods; the Centro Histórico and large expat community see little violent crime (Oct 2025: zero municipal homicides, calmest month in 20 years). Tourists are rarely targeted — but an Aug 2025 festival shooting (2 dead, 17 wounded) shows public-space exposure isn't zero, so the visitor modifier is generous (1.40, the model max) but not unbounded.
San Miguel de Allende.homicideSource: SESNSP via Mexico News Daily, 50th most violent municipality, 12mo to Aug 2025
